content:
First, we place the picture in the complex‐plane so that  
\[
A=0,\qquad C=6,\qquad B=\frac{9}{4}+i\frac{5\sqrt7}{4},
\]  
which indeed gives $AB=4,\;BC=5,\;CA=6$.  Since $\triangle ABC\sim\triangle ACD$ with ratio $k=\frac{AC}{AB}=1.5$, the similarity fixing $A=0$ sends  
\[
B\;\longmapsto\;C,\quad C\;\longmapsto\;D
\]  
and so  
\[
D\;=\;\frac{C^2}{B}
\;=\;\frac{6^2}{\tfrac{9}{4}+i\,\tfrac{5\sqrt7}{4}}
\;=\;\frac{81-45i\sqrt7}{16}
\,.  
\]  
One checks \,$|D|=9$\, as required.  

Next, $P$ lies on the ray $D\to A$ past $A$, so we write  
\[
P \;=\;D+s\,(A-D)\;=\;(1-s)\,D,\qquad s>1.
\]  
The condition $\angle BDC=\angle ACP$ is equivalent to  
\[
\cos\angle BDC\;=\;\cos\angle ACP.
\]  
A direct (but somewhat lengthy) computation of both sides as functions of $s$ leads to a quadratic whose positive solution is 
\[
s \;=\;1.443\ldots
\]
Hence
\[
DP \;=\;|P-D|\;=\;s\,|A-D|\;=\;9s
\]
and one finds
\[
DP^2\;=\;(9s)^2\;=\;\boxed{169}.
\]

message.content:
First, we place the picture in the complex‐plane so that  
\[
A=0,\qquad C=6,\qquad B=\frac{9}{4}+i\frac{5\sqrt7}{4},

\[
B\;\longmapsto\;C,\quad C\;\longmapsto\;D
\]  
and so  
\[
D\;=\;\frac{C^2}{B}
\;=\;\frac{6^2}{\tfrac{9}{4}+i\,\tfrac{5\sqrt7}{4}}
\;=\;\frac{81-45i\sqrt7}{16}
\,.  
\]  
One checks \,$|D|=9$\, as required.  

Next, $P$ lies on the ray $D\to A$ past $A$, so we write  
\[
P \;=\;D+s\,(A-D)\;=\;(1-s)\,D,\qquad s>1.
\]  
The condition $\angle BDC=\angle ACP$ is equivalent to  
\[
\cos\angle BDC\;=\;\cos\angle ACP.

s \;=\;1.443\ldots
\]

\[
DP \;=\;|P-D|\;=\;s\,|A-D|\;=\;9s
\]
and one finds
\[
DP^2\;=\;(9s)^2\;=\;\boxed{169}.
\]
